SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description1Txt: THE FOUNDATION OPERATES THE BELLAGIO CENTER AS A RESOURCE FOR FOSTERING DIALOGUE AND COLLABORATIVE PROBLEMS SOLVING FOR PARTICIPANTS AROUND THE WORLD SINCE 1959. THE CENTER HOSTS CONVENINGS THAT FOCUS ON TOPICS OF INTERNATIONAL IMPORTANCE, WITH PREFERENCE GIVEN TO CONVENINGS THAT ALIGN WITH THE FOUNDATION'S AREAS OF INTEREST. IN 2024, THE BELLAGIO CENTER HOSTED 62 CONVENINGS AND 111 RESIDENTS WITH A TOTAL ANNUAL HEADCOUNT OF APPROXIMATELY 1,448 PARTICIPANTS ACROSS BOTH PROGRAMS.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description2Txt: RF CATALYTIC CAPITAL, INC (RFCC) IS AN ORGANIZATION AFFILIATED WITH THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION AND ESTABLISHED TO SCALE NEW AND EXPANDED PARTNERSHIPS WITH A FOCUS ON IMPROVING THE LIVES OF VULNERABLE PEOPLE AROUND THE WORLD. RFCC'S ACTIVITY ALIGNS WITH AND IS INTENDED TO ADVANCE THE FOUNDATION'S INITIATIVES, INCLUDING THE POWER AND FOOD PORTFOLIOS. THE FOUNDATION MAINTAINS SIGNIFICANT INVOLVEMENT IN RFCC'S WORK, WHICH INCLUDES THE PROVISION OF TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO GRANTEES AND PARTNERS, SUPPORT FOR PROGRAM DESIGN AND STRATEGY DEVELOPMENT, AND CAPITAL MOBILIZATION.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description3Txt: SMART POWER FOR RURAL DEVELOPMENT INDIA FOUNDATION (SPI) WAS FORMED TO PROVIDE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO DEVELOPERS AND HELP EXPAND THE FIELD OF OFFGRID RENEWABLE ENERGY TO RURAL VILLAGES IN INDIA. SPI'S PILOT MINI-GRID PROJECT PROVIDED THE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE FOR FREE ON THE SITE SELECTIONS, DEMAND/CUSTOMER ACQUISITION AND PLANT DESIGN, LEADING TO CONCESSIONAL EQUITY CAPITAL AFTER THE PILOT PHASE IS COMPLETED AND SUCCESSFUL.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description4Txt: THE FOUNDATION OPERATES A CONVENING CENTER ON THE SECOND FLOOR OF THE BUILDING IN WHICH ITS NEW YORK CITY HEADQUARTERS IS LOCATED. THE CONVENING SPACE IS PROVIDED AT NO COST TO NON-PROFIT OR EQUIVALENT PARTNERS TO COLLABORATIVELY OR INDEPENDENTLY HOST EVENTS ADVANCING CHARITABLE WORK ALIGNED WITH THE FOUNDATION'S INITIATIVES. IN 2024, THE FOUNDATION'S SPACE WAS UTILIZED FOR 32 EVENTS.

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: 990PF, PART VI-B, LINE 5(D) - EXPENDITURE RESPONSIBILITY GRANTS AND PRIS
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE FOLLOWING TEXT RELATES TO THE EXPENDITURE RESPONSIBILITY STATEMENT, IN RESPONSE TO PART VI-B, LINE 5D. PROSPECTIVE GRANTEES ARE ASKED TO FURNISH INFORMATION IN ORDER TO ENABLE THE FOUNDATION TO DETERMINE WHETHER THEY HAVE RECEIVED A DETERMINATION OF 501(C)(3) STATUS FROM THE U.S. INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE OR MAY BE CONSIDERED THE EQUIVALENT OF A TAX-EXEMPT CHARITY, IF FORMED OUTSIDE THE UNITED STATES. PROSPECTIVE GRANTEES WITH RESPECT TO WHOM THE FOUNDATION IS BELIEVED TO HAVE "EXPENDITURE RESPONSIBILITY" OBLIGATIONS ARE INFORMED BY LETTER, BEFORE PAYMENT IS MADE ON A GRANT, OF THE RESPONSIBILITIES IMPOSED UPON THE FOUNDATION AND ITS GRANTEES BY THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE OF 1986, AS AMENDED. EACH GRANTEE MUST AGREE IN WRITING TO THOSE TERMS AND CONDITIONS, INCLUDING THE OBLIGATION TO PROVIDE FINANCIAL AND SUBSTANTIVE REPORTS ON HOW FOUNDATION GRANT FUNDS ARE EXPENDED. THE REPORTS THAT ARE RECEIVED ARE REVIEWED FOR GENERAL ACCURACY, SUFFICIENCY AND COMPLIANCE WITH THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE GRANT. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS REQUESTED FROM GRANTEES IN INSTANCES WHERE REPORTS ARE CONSIDERED INADEQUATE. WHERE THE FOUNDATION HAS NOT RECEIVED FINANCIAL OR SUBSTANTIVE REPORTS FROM A GRANTEE WHEN EXPECTED, FOUNDATION STAFF FOLLOW UP IN SEVERAL WAYS, INCLUDING REMINDERS THAT ARE SENT AT REGULAR INTERVALS AFTER REPORTS ARE EXPECTED AND PERSONAL FOLLOW-UP BY PROGRAM AND GRANTS MANAGEMENT STAFF THROUGH TELEPHONE, MAIL AND E-MAIL.THE STATEMENT LISTS GRANT AND PROGRAM-RELATED INVESTMENT EXPENDITURES WITH RESPECT TO WHICH THE FOUNDATION HAS EXERCISED EXPENDITURE RESPONSIBILITY. WHERE THE GRANTEE'S NAME IS FOLLOWED BY AN ASTERISK (*), THE GRANTEE ORGANIZATION IS EITHER A PRIVATE FOUNDATION, A PRIVATE OPERATING FOUNDATION, OR A FOREIGN PRIVATE-FOUNDATION EQUIVALENT. ALL OTHER GRANTEES ARE NON-501(C)(3) ORGANIZATIONS OR ORGANIZATIONS FOR WHICH THE FOUNDATION HAS NOT MADE A REASONABLE DETERMINATION OF 501(C)(3) EQUIVALENCY STATUS THAT RECEIVED GRANTS FOR CHARITABLE PROJECTS AND MAINTAINED PAYMENTS IN SEGREGATED FUNDS AS REQUIRED BY TREASURY REG. 53.4945-6(C).IF A PARTICULAR GRANT EXPENDITURE IS SHOWN TO HAVE BEEN FULLY EXPENDED BY THE GRANTEE BUT THE FOUNDATION HAS NOT YET RECEIVED ONE OR MORE REPORTS, THE FOUNDATION WILL REPORT ON THE STATUS OF THE EXPENDITURE ON OUR 2024 990-PF. UNLESS OTHERWISE NOTED, THE REPORTS RECEIVED WERE FOUND TO BE IN ORDER AND TO SHOW THAT THE FUNDS WERE FULLY EXPENDED FOR THE PURPOSE OF THE GRANT.REPORTING STATUS IS AS OF 6/30/2025.

(A) THE FOUNDATION DID NOT ACCEPT UNSOLICITED INQUIRIES IN 2024 EXCEPT FOR APPLICANTS TO THE BELLAGIO CENTER, ITALY, AND BIG BETS FELLOWSHIPS PROGRAMS. (B) APPLICANTS TO THE BELLAGIO CENTER, ITALY, AND FELLOWSHIP PROGRAMS ARE ASKED TO APPLY ONLINE THROUGH PLATFORMS LINKED FROM WWW.ROCKEFELLERFOUNDATION.ORG. (C) IN 2024, THERE WERE NO SPECIFIC SUBMISSION DEADLINES FOR THE FOUNDATION'S PROGRAMS, OTHER THAN FOR THE BELLAGIO AND FELLOWSHIP PROGRAMS. IN ADDITION, FROM TIME TO TIME, REQUESTS FOR PROPOSALS WERE SENT OUT WITH SPECIFIC DEADLINES TO A SELECT GROUP OF PROSPECTIVE GRANTEES FOR PARTICULAR PROJECTS.(D) THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION DEVELOPS ITS PROGRAMS, STRATEGIES AND APPROACHES DESIGNED TO HAVE AN IMPACT ON A PARTICULAR SET OF ISSUES OR CHALLENGES THROUGH A CONSULTATIVE AND COLLABORATIVE PROCESS. WE ENGAGE A VARIETY OF GROUPS, INCLUDING SUBJECT MATTER EXPERTS, POLICYMAKERS AND REPRESENTATIVES OF APPLICABLE COMMUNITIES TO FULLY UNDERSTAND A GIVEN CHALLENGE OR OPPORTUNITY, INCLUDING THE ECONOMIC, POLITICAL OR SOCIAL CONTEXT IN WHICH IT RESIDES. IN THE COURSE OF PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT, THE FOUNDATION IDENTIFIES AND CONSIDERS ORGANIZATIONS THAT WE BELIEVE ARE BEST POSITIONED TO IMPLEMENT DEFINED COMPONENTS OF A PROGRAM. THIS PROCESS HELPS DETERMINE THE ORGANIZATIONS TO WHICH THE FOUNDATION WILL PROVIDE GRANTS, AND WE WILL THEN REQUEST PROJECT PROPOSALS FROM THOSE ORGANIZATIONS. IN ORDER TO FOCUS AND MAXIMIZE THE IMPACT OF OUR GRANTMAKING, WE RARELY PROVIDE FUNDING IN RESPONSE TO UNSOLICITED PROPOSALS. AS A MATTER OF POLICY, THE FOUNDATION DOES NOT GIVE OR LEND MONEY FOR PERSONAL AID TO INDIVIDUALS OR, EXCEPT IN RARE CASES, FUND ENDOWMENTS, OR CONTRIBUTE TO BUILDING FUNDS.DURING 2024, THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PURSUED ITS 1913 MISSION OF PROMOTING THE WELL-BEING OF HUMANITY THROUGHOUT THE WORLD BY BUILDING COLLABORATIVE PARTNERSHIPS AT THE FRONTIERS OF SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, AND INNOVATION TO ENABLE INDIVIDUALS, FAMILIES, AND COMMUNITIES TO FLOURISH. THE FOUNDATION WORKS TO PROMOTE THE WELL-BEING OF HUMANITY AND MAKES OPPORTUNITY UNIVERSAL. CURRENT WORK WITHIN THESE INITIATIVES, AS WELL AS AREAS BEING EXPLORED FOR FUTURE INITIATIVES, ARE DESCRIBED ON THE FOUNDATION'S WEBSITE AT WWW.ROCKEFELLERFOUNDATION.ORG, WHICH IS CONTINUALLY UPDATED. ACTIVE WORK DURING 2024 INCLUDED: BIG BETS FELLOWSHIPS, SCALING RENEWABLE ENERGY FOR ALL, STIMULATING ECONOMIC MOBILITY, AND ENSURING EQUITABLE ACCESS TO HEALTHY AND NUTRITIOUS FOOD.THE FOUNDATION ALSO OPERATES A CONFERENCE AND STUDY PROGRAM AT ITS BELLAGIO CENTER IN ITALY, AND CONFERENCE SPACE AT THE NYC OFFICE.

WITH REGARD TO PART XIV SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION REFLECTING GRANTS AND CONTRIBUTIONS PAID DURING THE YEAR AND APPROVED FOR FUTURE PAYMENT, ORGANIZATIONS GIVEN THE FOUNDATION STATUS CODE DESIGNATION "PC" (OR PUBLIC CHARITY) INCLUDE U.S. PUBLIC CHARITIES DESCRIBED IN SECTION509(A)(1) OR (2) OF THE CODE AND ORGANIZATIONS BASED OUTSIDE THE U.S. THAT HAVE BEEN FOUND TO BE THE EQUIVALENT OF A U.S. PUBLIC CHARITY.
